problem: How many ways can 1995 be factored as a product of two two-digit numbers? (Two factorizations of the form $a\cdot b$ and $b\cdot a$ are considered the same).

classified_questions.question: What is the prime factorization of 1995?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many two-digit numbers are divisible by 19 and can be multiplied with another two-digit number to get 1995?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any two-digit numbers that are factors of 1995 and cannot be paired with another two-digit number to get 1995? If so, why?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can 1995 be factored into the product of three two-digit numbers? If not, why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Would the answer be different if we allowed the factorizations to be in the form of $a\cdot b$ and $b\cdot a$ separately? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can the same method be used to find the number of ways a different integer can be factored into the product of two two-digit numbers? If so, how would the process differ?
classified_questions.label: b
